lede: A look at January 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Sophia Reid-Gantzert and Zeeko Zaki, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2026 Adamuz rail disaster
events.desc: At least 45 people are killed and 292 others injured after two trains collide in Adamuz in the worst railway disaster in over a decade in Spain.

events.title: Tlahuelilpan pipeline explosion
events.desc: An oil pipeline explosion near Tlahuelilpan, Hidalgo, Mexico, kills 137 people.

events.title: Sierra Leone Civil War
events.desc: The Sierra Leone Civil War is declared over.

events.title: International Olympic Committee
events.desc: The International Olympic Committee restores Jim Thorpe's Olympic medals to his family.

events.title: Christianity in Lebanon
events.desc: Lebanese Christian militias kill at least 1,000 in Karantina, Beirut.

events.title: Willie O'Ree
events.desc: Willie O'Ree, the first Black Canadian National Hockey League player, makes his NHL debut with the Boston Bruins.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: British troops launch a general counter-offensive against Italian East Africa.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Paris Peace Conference opens in Versailles, France.

events.title: First Fleet
events.desc: The first elements of the First Fleet carrying 736 convicts from Great Britain to Australia arrive at Botany Bay.

events.title: Leo II (emperor)
events.desc: Seven-year-old Leo II succeeds his maternal grandfather Leo I as Byzantine emperor. He dies ten months later.

births.name: Syl Apps
births.line: Canadian pole vaulter, ice hockey player, and politician (died 1998)

births.name: Sam Zemurray
births.line: Russian-American businessman, founded the Cuyamel Fruit Company (died 1961)

deaths.text: Claire van Kampen, English director and composer
Michael Botmang, Nigerian politician, 17th Governor of Plateau State
Jan van Riebeeck, Dutch politician, founded Cape Town

observances.text: Christian feast day: Amy Carmichael (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Athanasius of Alexandria (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Confession of Peter (Eastern Orthodox, some Anglican and Lutheran Churches)
